channel.title: Storage on Dijure | Digital Architectures with a hint of Java

item.title: Storage with Redis
item.description:
Our microservices often need fast and relatively local data storage. Redis (REmote DIctionary Server) is an open source, in-memory, NoSQL key/value store that is used primarily as an application cache or quick-response database. It’s recognized for its speed, reliability, and features. It’s a popular choice for developers because it’s easy to hook to our applications for solving the data storage designs.

These next steps provide an ideal place to learn how to install and connect to a Redis application on Kubernetes.
